questions.question: What can be used to construct circles and their parts?
questions.explanation: A compass is an instrument that allows us to draw circles and arcs.

questions.question: How can point A be located without trial and error in the 'House' problem?
questions.correct_answer: Using a circle with radius 5 cm centered at B or C
questions.explanation: Drawing a circle with radius 5 cm from point B or C helps locate point A.

questions.question: What is the property of opposite sides in a rectangle?
questions.explanation: In a rectangle, opposite sides have the same length.

questions.question: How can we draw the upper and lower curves of an eye?
questions.correct_answer: Using a compass with a fixed radius
questions.explanation: The tip of the compass is placed at points A and B to draw the curves.

questions.question: What are the angles in a rectangle?
questions.explanation: All four angles in a rectangle measure 90 degrees each.

questions.question: Can we construct a 4-sided figure with all equal sides but not a square?
questions.explanation: An equilateral quadrilateral has all equal sides, but it's not a square.

questions.question: How can we find the minimum distance between points X and Y in rectangle ABCD?
questions.correct_answer: By measuring the length of XY
questions.explanation: The shortest distance between X and Y is when they are closest to each other on their respective sides.

questions.question: What is the relationship between AB and the minimum distance XY?
questions.correct_answer: XY is less than or equal to half of AB
questions.explanation: The minimum distance XY cannot be greater than half of the length of AB.

questions.question: Can we use a compass to measure distances in rectangle ABCD?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, by marking points X and Y with the compass tip
questions.explanation: Using the compass, we can mark the positions of X and Y on their respective sides.

questions.question: How can we record multiple measurements of XY for different positions of X and Y?
questions.correct_answer: By listing the distance of X from A, distance of Y from B, and length of XY
questions.explanation: We can use a table to keep track of the lengths XY for various positions of X and Y.

important_notes.title: Properties of Rectangles
important_notes.content: Rectangles have four right angles (90°) and opposite sides that are equal in length.

important_notes.title: Construction with Compasses
important_notes.content: Compasses can be used to draw circles, arcs, and mark points for various constructions.

important_notes.title: Equilateral Quadrilaterals
important_notes.content: An equilateral quadrilateral is a four-sided figure with all sides equal in length but not necessarily a square.

important_notes.title: Minimum Distance XY
important_notes.content: The minimum distance between X and Y occurs when they are closest to each other on their respective sides.

important_notes.title: Relationship Between AB and XY
important_notes.content: XY is less than or equal to half of the length of AB in rectangle ABCD.

important_notes.title: Using Compasses for Measurements
important_notes.content: Compasses can be used to mark points X and Y on their respective sides, allowing us to measure distances.

important_notes.title: Recording Multiple Measurements
important_notes.content: We can use a table to record multiple measurements of XY for different positions of X and Y.

important_notes.title: Properties of Circles
important_notes.content: Circles have equal radii and all points on the circumference are equidistant from the center.

important_notes.title: Drawing Eyes with Compasses
important_notes.content: The upper and lower curves of an eye can be drawn using a compass with a fixed radius.

important_notes.title: Construction of Rectangles
important_notes.content: Rectangles can be constructed by drawing two opposite sides and then joining the other two vertices.

facts.fact: A rectangle has four right angles (90°) and opposite sides that are equal in length.
Compasses can be used to draw circles, arcs, and mark points for various constructions.
An equilateral quadrilateral is a four-sided figure with all sides equal in length but not necessarily a square.
The minimum distance between X and Y occurs when they are closest to each other on their respective sides.
XY is less than or equal to half of the length of AB in rectangle ABCD.
Compasses can be used to mark points X and Y on their respective sides, allowing us to measure distances.
We can use a table to record multiple measurements of XY for different positions of X and Y.
Circles have equal radii and all points on the circumference are equidistant from the center.
The upper and lower curves of an eye can be drawn using a compass with a fixed radius.
Rectangles can be constructed by drawing two opposite sides and then joining the other two vertices.
